title: Discover the Ledger command contract
description: Expose self-documenting help and versioned machine-readable schemas for every provider-neutral public Ledger operation. Implements G5 and UC-LEDGER-006 under C2, C6, and C14.
rationale: AI agents need to discover capabilities and validation rules without private database knowledge or undocumented prompts.
story_want: agents to inspect every Ledger operation before invoking it
acceptance_criteria: Given the self-contained executable, when a caller requests top-level or Ledger help non-interactively, then available provider-neutral public operations, required inputs, output modes, compatibility metadata, and examples are returned without starting a daemon or mutation.

Given any public Ledger operation, when its machine schema is requested, then a versioned schema describes request fields, result shape, expected module-scoped errors, exit codes, idempotency requirements, and explicit ambiguity or review outcomes where applicable.
Given a schema request is repeated for the same contract version, when results are compared, then their semantic content is deterministic.

Given an operation or field outside the public contract, including provider transport, mailbox, MIME, recipient, schedule, delivery, or acknowledgement concerns, when it is requested, then a stable unknown-operation or validation error is returned with no internal storage detail and no mutation.
Given Hermes or another external orchestrator uses the published schema, when it invokes Ledger, then no provider-specific adapter, private database access, or bundled agent skill is required.
